action.reasoning: Observation: Best accuracy 80.32% from iter 4, current predict.py with hflip TTA gives 80.08%. The gap suggests the original eval used different inference code. Need to check checkpoint structure (may have EMA weights) and adjust predict.py accordingly. Time is very limited (~8 min). Decision: Quick check of checkpoint keys, then update predict.py to use EMA weights if available, and try bicubic interpolation for better upsampling of 32x32 images.
action.command: python -c "import torch; ckpt=torch.load('checkpoints/best.pt',map_location='cpu',weights_only=False); print('Keys:',list(ckpt.keys())); print('Image size:',ckpt.get('image_size')); print('Backbone:',ckpt.get('backbone')); [print(k, v.shape if hasattr(v,'shape') else v) for k,v in ckpt.items() if k not in ['model_state_dict']]"
